description:
You are a technical writer getting a set of draft Markdown documents ready for publication.  
    In your home directory there is already a folder named “/home/user/work_in_progress” that contains **exactly three** files:

        /home/user/work_in_progress/overview.md
        /home/user/work_in_progress/install.md
        /home/user/work_in_progress/faq.md

    Your job is to stage these drafts for release.

    1. Create a new directory named “/home/user/doc_publication”.  
    2. Move **all** the “*.md” files that currently reside in “/home/user/work_in_progress/” into this new “doc_publication” directory.  
    3. Inside “/home/user/doc_publication” create a plain-text log file called “filelist.log”.  
       • “filelist.log” must list **only** the names of the Markdown files (not their paths) that now reside in “/home/user/doc_publication”.  
       • The names must be in **strict alphabetical order**, one file per line, with a final trailing newline after the last filename.  
         Example of the exact required format (for illustration only):  
             faq.md⏎  
             install.md⏎  
             overview.md⏎  
       • Do **not** add any extra spaces, bullet symbols, or commentary.  
    4. Finally, display the contents of “filelist.log” to the terminal so it is visible in the session output.

    The automated checker will verify:
      • /home/user/doc_publication exists.  
      • Each Markdown file has been moved (not copied) from “work_in_progress” into “doc_publication”.  
      • “filelist.log” exists in “doc_publication” and its contents follow the exact format described above.  

    You can accomplish everything with about three or four standard shell commands.  
    Remember to work inside the Linux terminal only; no need for root privileges.
truth:
Initial filesystem state before the agent starts:

    Directory: /home/user/work_in_progress
        ├── overview.md
        │     Contents (3 lines):
        │         # Project Overview
        │         This document provides an overview of the project.
        │         TODO: expand details.
        ├── install.md
        │     Contents (4 lines):
        │         # Installation Guide
        │         Follow these steps to install the application.
        │         Step 1: ...
        │         TODO: add screenshots.
        └── faq.md
              Contents (2 lines):
                  # FAQ
                  Frequently asked questions will go here.

    Directory /home/user/doc_publication does **not** exist at start.

    After correct completion the filesystem must look like:

    /home/user/work_in_progress               → empty (no .md files present)
    /home/user/doc_publication/
        ├── overview.md      (same content as original)
        ├── install.md       (same content as original)
        ├── faq.md           (same content as original)
        └── filelist.log     (exactly 3 lines + trailing newline)
              Contents (including trailing newline):
                  faq.md
                  install.md
                  overview.md

    The automated test will read /home/user/doc_publication/filelist.log and compare its exact byte sequence to:
        "faq.md\ninstall.md\noverview.md\n"

    The test will also ensure that /home/user/work_in_progress contains no *.md files and that the three markdown files now reside inside /home/user/doc_publication.
